name: rtl-impl
description: RTL implementation: read MAS → header → registers → FSM → datapath → interrupt → memory → lint

tasks.detail: Read <module>_mas.md. Extract from each section: §2 Module Hierarchy + port list (name/width/direction/clock domain), §3 Feature Operation + FSM state table, §4 Register FAM (offset/name/width/access/reset), §5 Interrupt sources + enable/status/clear logic, §6 Memory instances (type/depth/width/latency), §8 RTL Notes. Do not write any code yet.

tasks.content: Write module header: parameters and port declarations
tasks.detail: module <name> #(params) (ports); Match §2 exactly. All ports with explicit direction+type+width. Add `default_nettype none at top.

Implement all registers from MAS §4 FAM table. RW registers: written on APB/AXI write. RO registers: driven by internal logic. RW1C registers (interrupt status): cleared when CPU writes 1. Nonblocking (<=) in always_ff with synchronous rst_n.
Implement FSM exactly as defined in MAS §3 state table. One always_ff for state register, one always_comb for next-state + output logic. All states must be reachable. Add default clause.
Implement each feature's datapath as described in MAS §3. Blocking (=) in always_comb. Default assignment at top of each block to prevent latches. Case statements with default.
Implement all interrupt sources from MAS §5 table. irq output = OR of (INT_STATUS[i] & INT_ENABLE[i]) for all sources. W1C clear: INT_STATUS[i] <= INT_STATUS[i] & ~apb_wdata[i] on write.
Instantiate all memories from MAS §6 table. Use appropriate type (SRAM macro / FIFO). Verify latency matches §6 and timing matches §7.
Run /lint <module>.sv. Fix all errors. Warnings: fix width mismatches; document others with // lint_waive comments.
